description.p: Ever wanted to play a cool dragon slayer who leaps into the sky, decimating giant monsters in the blink of an eye? If so, the Wyrmslayer is for you. Sink your jaws into this package and build yourself a character that makes the others at the table relieved to have you on their side.

Injected by their hateful doctrine, Wyrmslayers are taught to point their physical and emotional power towards their most despised mortal enemy, dragonkind. Raised in resolute dogma, the ultimate goal of a Wyrmslayer is to complete a ritual known as The Sundering. This sacred text has been passed down from generation to generation as a well guarded secret and it is the premise on which this powerful faction rides.
Consume the essence of all that dragonkind offers and wield their elemental power against them. Raise the Exsanguinator's Spear at the peak of the great mountain and tear the sky aflame.
We will rend their wings. Our Terror will be theirs. The Sundering Sings. The Wyrmslayer's prayers

Below you will find some recommended ideas to help you get into the swing of what playing a wyrmslayer should be and feel like. Wyrmslayer's are combat focused. Dragons are a wyrmslayer's mortal enemy so flying is considered taboo. Instead, wyrmslayers rely on their signature leap, allowing them to combat aerial opponents whilst representing the constraints of mortalkind.

You rely on your powerful mobility to control the battlefield, leaping from creature to creature and keeping the enemy off balance. You often seek out the largest foe possible, whether to protect your allies from them or to take them down yourself. You rely on your unique ability to immediately reposition and create new angles and advantages through creative movement.

You study the habitats of dangerous creatures, constantly assessing vantage points and finding ways to utilise the environment to your advantage. You seek out powerful relics that will assist in your ability to defeat powerful foes, particularly weapons to fuse with your wyrmslayer arsenal.

To create a wyrmslayer, consult the following subsections which give you hit points, proficiencies and starting equipment. Then look at the table to see the features you gain at each level. The descriptions of those features appear in the "Class Features" section.

You can make a wyrmslayer quickly by following these suggestions. First, put your highest ability in Strength (if you want to focus on certain melee weapons) or Dexterity (if you want to focus on certain ranged or finesse weapons. Your second highest ability score should be Constitution and your third should be either Dexterity or Strength, depending on which stat you made your highest. Next, choose a background that gives proficiency in skills such as Athletics, Aerobatics, Perception, Survival or Medicine.
As an wyrmslayer, you gain the following class features.

ul.li: Hit Points at 1st Level: 8 + your Constitution modifier
Hit Points at Higher Levels: 1d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per wyrmslayer level after 1st
Skills: Choose three from Acrobatics, Athletics, Perception, Survival, Stealth and Insight

description.p: You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background:

ul.li: (a) two martial weapons, (b) one martial weapon and simple weapon or (c) one wyrmslayer weapon.
(a) chain shirt or (b) leather armour, a longbow, and 20 arrows
(a) dungeoneer's pack or (b) an adventurer's pack
Alternatively, you can forgo this starting equipment and the equipment granted by your background, instead starting with Sd4 x 10 gp.

tr.td: Enhanced Leap, Leap Strike, Fighting Style

description.p: Some features require a save and the DC is equal to 8 + proficiency + Constitution modifier.

sheet.description: Your DC for Wyrmslayer features is {{wyrmslayer:dc}}

description.p: Specialising in a unique methods of hunting dragons, the legendary smith work of the Wyrmslayer Conclave grants you access to a powerful arsenal of dragon hunting weapons. Choose a weapon from the list below and become proficient with it. You gain an additional weapon proficiency level 10. These weapon's do not fall under the simple or martial category and require special Wyrmslayer training to wield, meaning non wyrmslayer characters cannot use them effectively. Wyrmslayer weapons can fuse with and absorb the powers of other magical weapons of their corresponding type, as shown below in the list.

tr.td: Deals extra d6 vs Huge or larger monsters
1d4 on enemies beside target on hit
When attacking, can use a bonus attack
melee attacks on creatures within 15ft with no penalties. 2d4 beyond 10ft. No unmounted penalty.
Can grapple enemy on hit. 1d4 extra slashing when hit by melee attacks while grappled
1d8 blugeoning using pole or 1d6 using line

pole is melee, line is ranged. Line can grapple on hit and pull as bonus action 10ft toward you. You can extend the line to 60 ft, but unhooks beyond that. No size limits on grapple, but pull is limited to 4 feet if they are more than 1 size larger than you.
Prevents regaining hit points on hit until end of their next turn

description.p: When a Wyrmslayer weapon absorbs another weapon, it gains the magical properties, effects, bonuses and powers of the weapon it merges with, including any curses. Whilst in this form, the Wyrmslayer weapon retains its own abilities and traits, such as light, thrown or finesse. It also retains it's base damage die, unless the absorbed weapon contains a property to increase it. Wyrmslayer weapons can only merge with a weapon that matches their category. For example, a Greatspear cannot absorb the properties of a magical sword or axe and instead, must inherit the powers of a polearm.

A Wyrmslayer weapon can only have one fusion at any given time. You can remove the fusion component and return both weapons to their original form whenever you please. Fusing a weapon requires 10 minutes and removing a weapon takes 1 minute. If the fused weapon requires attunement, you must follow the s tandard attunement rules after fusing to wield the weapon effectively. The weapon's appearance changes to create an amalgamation of its original look and that of its component.

sheet.description: These weapon's do not fall under the simple or martial category and require special Wyrmslayer training to wield, meaning non wyrmslayer characters cannot use them effectively. Wyrmslayer weapons can fuse with and absorb the powers of other magical weapons of their corresponding type. You can infuse your weapon with a compatible weapon and it gains the magical properties, effects, bonuses, and any powers of the weapon it merges with, including curses and attunement requirements. Fusing takes 10 minutes and removing takes 1 minute.

description.p: At first level, your innate draconic powers are unleashed and you've learned to specialise in one of the chromatic elements. Choose an element between Fire, Cold, Lightning, Acid or Poison and gain resistance to that element. In addition, when you slay a powerful dragon (GM's discretion), you can absorb its essence, adding its corresponding element to your resilience pool. You can only have one of these resistances active at a time with this feature and can switch between your existing choices during a short or long rest. Your GM may also allow you alternate ways to collect these elements, based on your campaign setting.
You are resistant to fire.

sheet.description: when you slay a powerful dragon (GM's discretion), you can absorb its essence, adding its corresponding element to your resilience pool. You can only have one of these resistances active at a time with this feature and can switch between your existing choices during a short or long rest. Your GM may also allow you alternate ways to collect these elements, based on your campaign setting.

description.p: You are resistant to cold.

sheet.description: when you slay a powerful dragon (GM's discretion), you can absorb its essence, adding its corresponding element to your resilience pool. You can only have one of these resistances active at a time with this feature and can switch between your existing choices during a short or long rest. Your GM may also allow you alternate ways to collect these elements, based on your campaign setting.

description.p: You are resistant to lightning.

sheet.description: when you slay a powerful dragon (GM's discretion), you can absorb its essence, adding its corresponding element to your resilience pool. You can only have one of these resistances active at a time with this feature and can switch between your existing choices during a short or long rest. Your GM may also allow you alternate ways to collect these elements, based on your campaign setting.

description.p: You are resistant to acid.

sheet.description: when you slay a powerful dragon (GM's discretion), you can absorb its essence, adding its corresponding element to your resilience pool. You can only have one of these resistances active at a time with this feature and can switch between your existing choices during a short or long rest. Your GM may also allow you alternate ways to collect these elements, based on your campaign setting.

description.p: You are resistant to poison.

sheet.description: when you slay a powerful dragon (GM's discretion), you can absorb its essence, adding its corresponding element to your resilience pool. You can only have one of these resistances active at a time with this feature and can switch between your existing choices during a short or long rest. Your GM may also allow you alternate ways to collect these elements, based on your campaign setting.

description.p: Your draconic power manifests in its most iconic form, surging your body with enhanced physical capabilities. At second level, on each of your turns, you can leap a distance of 20ft as part of your movement. The leap does not require an action, however it always uses half of your total movement and can only be used once per turn. When leaping from difficult terrain your leap distance is halved. If you are unable to move, you are unable to use this feature. The distance of your enhanced leap increases as you gain levels in this class, as shown on the Wyrmslayer class table. You do not take damage whilst falling from your enhanced leap distance.

sheet.description: On each of your turns, you can leap a distance of {{enhanced-leap:distance}}ft as part of your movement. The leap does not require an action, however it always uses half of your total movement and can only be used once per turn. When leaping from difficult terrain your leap distance is halved. If you are unable to move, you are unable to use this feature.You do not take damage whilst falling from your enhanced leap distance.

description.p: At second level, when you take the Attack action, you can incorporate it with your Enhanced Leap. Choose a creature within your leaping distance leap towards them, making a melee weapon attack in the process.

This attack deals bonus damage and allows you to reposition yourself anywhere within 5ft of the target as part of your landing whether it hits or not. Note that you cannot use Leap Strike if you have already used your enhanced leap for movement. The amount of extra damage increases as you gain levels in this class, as shown in the Leap Damage column of the wyrmslayer class table.

sheet.description: When you take the Attack action, you can incorporate it with your Enhanced Leap. Choose a creature within your leaping distance leap towards them, making a melee weapon attack in the process. This attack deals 1d{{leap-strike:damage}} bonus damage and allows you to reposition yourself anywhere within 5ft of the target as part of your landing whether it hits or not. Note that you cannot use Leap Strike if you have already used your enhanced leap for movement.

description.p: At second level, you adopt a style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the fighting style options (see core rules). You can't take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again. You can choose an extra fighting style when you reach 10th level.
At third level, you adapt to your preferred method of hunting. Your chosen conclave grants you features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 10th, 15th and 18th level.
You have a choice of Sky Splitters, Toll Sentinels, or Death Singers.

When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

sheet.description: You can attack twice when you take the attack action on your turn.

description.p: Your innate draconic powers have transferred from a protective state to one that does harm. Starting at 6th level, your Leap Strike bonus damage uses whichever element you've chosen as part of the Draconic Resilience feature. If you have more than one type of Draconic resistance available, you may choose the damage type, regardless of which resistance you have active.

sheet.description: Your Leap Strike bonus damage uses whichever element you've chosen as part of the Draconic Resilience feature. If you have more than one type of Draconic resistance available, you may choose the damage type, regardless of which resistance you have active.

description.p: Starting at 9th level, when you take the Attack action as part of Enhanced Leap, you can choose a location instead of a creature as your target. Each creature you choose within a 10 ft radius of your attack must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your save DC (8 + your proficiency + your Constitution modifier) or be knocked prone. Creatures who fail the save take full damage, as though they're subject to a weapon attack plus your Leap Strike Die, and creatures who make the save take half damage instead. You may choose a damage type from your list of Draconic Resiliences for this abilities damage. You can use this feature twice and regain all charges upon completing a long rest.

sheet.description: When you take the Attack action as part of Enhanced Leap, you can choose a location instead of a creature as your target. Each creature you choose within a 10 ft radius of your attack must succeed on a DC {{cataclysmic-impact:dc}} Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. Creatures who fail the save take full damage, as though they're subject to a weapon attack plus your Leap Strike Die, and creatures who make the save take half damage instead. You may choose a damage type from your list of Draconic Resiliences for this abilities damage.

description.p: Wyrmslayers relish in challenging powerful foes and hold otherworldly grudges towards their adversaries. Starting at 11th level, as a bonus action, you may designate a target by uttering a creature's name you are aware of or choosing one you can see. When you do so, the creature becomes your mortal enemy and you gain the following effects against them.

ul.li: You can always sense each others presence within half a mile, though without an exact location.
You have advantage on any ability checks used to locate them or seek information about them.
When your mortal enemy dies, heal a number of hit points equal to your Wyrmslayer class level. If you cannot regain hit points, gain temporary hit points equal to your Wyrmslayer class level instead.

description.p: When you declare a creature as your mortal enemy, they become aware of you and immediately recognise your hostility. A creature stays as your mortal enemy until either it dies, you change your mortal enemy to another creature or you choose to end this effect. You may only have one creature as your mortal enemy at a time. You can use this feature once and must finish a long rest before you can use it again.

sheet.description: As a bonus action, you may designate a target by uttering a creature's name you are aware of or choosing one you can see. When you do so, the creature becomes your mortal enemy and you gain the following effects against them. -You can always sense each others presence within half a mile, though without an exact location. -You have advantage on any ability checks used to locate them or seek information about them. -When your mortal enemy dies, heal {{level:wyrmslayer}} hit points. If you cannot regain hit points, gain temporary hit points instead. When you declare a creature as your mortal enemy, they become aware of you and immediately recognise your hostility. A creature stays as your mortal enemy until either it dies, you change your mortal enemy to another creature or you choose to end this effect. You may only have one creature as your mortal enemy at a time.

description.p: You've mastered your momentum when leaping, able to switch between targets with ease. Starting at 13th level, When you use Enhanced Leap as part of a weapon attack, you can use the remainder of your leap distance to make your extra attack against another creature within the remaining range without provoking any opportunity attacks. You then land within 5ft of your second target as normal whether you have movement left or not.

sheet.description: When you use Enhanced Leap as part of a weapon attack, you can use the remainder of your leap distance to make your extra attack against another creature within the remaining range without provoking any opportunity attacks. You then land within 5ft of your second target as normal whether you have movement left or not.

description.p: Starting at 14th level, Choose another element between Thunder, Necrotic, Radiant or Psychic and gain resistance to it. You may now have two Draconic Resiliences active at the same time.

sheet.description: You may now have two Draconic Resiliences active at the same time.

description.p: You are resistant to thunder. You may now have two Draconic Resiliences active at the same time.

sheet.description: You may now have two Draconic Resiliences active at the same time.

description.p: You are resistant to necrotic. You may now have two Draconic Resiliences active at the same time.

sheet.description: You may now have two Draconic Resiliences active at the same time.

description.p: You are resistant to radiant. You may now have two Draconic Resiliences active at the same time.

sheet.description: You may now have two Draconic Resiliences active at the same time.

description.p: You are resistant to psychic. You may now have two Draconic Resiliences active at the same time.

sheet.description: You may now have two Draconic Resiliences active at the same time.

description.p: Your grudges deepen and your hatred festers as your adversaries cower in fear at your challenge. Starting at 17th level, you gain additional effects against your mortal enemy.

ul.li: If the creature is huge, gargantuan or colossal you gain advantage on all attacks against them.
If you're reduced to O hit points whilst in combat your mortal enemy, you can choose to drop to one hit point instead, one time per mortal enemy.

sheet.description: You gain additional effects against your mortal enemy. -If the creature is huge, gargantuan or colossal you gain advantage on all attacks against them. -If you're reduced to 0 hit points whilst in combat your mortal enemy, you can choose to drop to one hit point instead, one time per mortal enemy.

description.p: At 20th level, your weapon attacks deal Force damage. Additionally, your Draconic Resilience and Draconic Power feature can now also be Force Damage.

sheet.description: Your weapon attacks deal Force damage. Additionally, your Draconic Resilience and Draconic Power feature can now also be Force Damage.

description.p: This weapon deals double damage to structures instead of half.
When you hit with a weapon attack, you can push the creature 5ft as part of the attack.
When you make an attack using the thrown property, the weapon returns to your hand afterwards.
This weapon counts as a shield as well as a weapon, with its own specified armour class (AC) bonus. You cannot wield a second shield for increased AC.

element.name: Weapon Proficiency (Greatspear)
element.supports: Weapon, Melee, ID_INTERNAL_DAMAGE_TYPE_PIERCING, ID_INTERNAL_WEAPON_PROPERTY_REACH,, ID_INTERNAL_WEAPON_PROPERTY_FINESSE, wyrmslayer-weapons, ID_INTERNAL_WEAPON_GROUP_WYRMSLAYER_WEAPONS

description.p: A large yet lightweight spear with greater reach than normal. Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.
